meta.description: Coaching tips for Level 7: Workflow Orchestration

tips.tip: If you've typed the same kind of prompt 3+ times, it should be a Custom Command. Save it in .claude/commands/.
tips.example_before: Every time: 'Create a new React component with TypeScript, tests, and Storybook story in src/components/...'
tips.example_after: Create .claude/commands/new-component.md with the template. Now just type /new-component ComponentName.

tech_variants.react: Create /new-component for React components with TypeScript, tests, and Storybook stories.
tech_variants.python: Create /new-endpoint for FastAPI routes with Pydantic models, tests, and OpenAPI docs.
tech_variants.go: Create /new-handler for HTTP handlers with middleware, tests, and OpenAPI annotations.

tips.tip: Good commands are parameterized with $ARGUMENTS — they define the workflow framework, AI fills in the details.
tips.example_before: Command that's too specific: 'Create a UserProfile component with name, email, avatar fields'
tips.example_after: Command uses $ARGUMENTS: 'Create a component for $ARGUMENTS. Follow project conventions, add tests, add to barrel exports.'

tips.tip: Target 5+ commands covering your daily workflows: new feature, bug fix, refactor, add tests, code review.
tips.example_after: /new-feature, /fix-bug, /add-tests, /refactor, /code-review — each with project-specific conventions baked in.

tips.tip: Configure Hooks to auto-run type checking or linting after AI edits files — catch errors before you even review.
tips.example_before: AI edits 5 files, you run tsc manually, find 3 type errors, go back to fix
tips.example_after: Hook in settings.json: after file edit → run tsc --noEmit. Type errors caught immediately, AI fixes them in the same session.

tech_variants.react: PostToolUse hook: run 'tsc --noEmit' after edits to catch TypeScript errors immediately.
tech_variants.python: PostToolUse hook: run 'mypy --strict' after edits to catch type errors immediately.
tech_variants.go: PostToolUse hook: run 'go vet ./...' after edits to catch issues immediately.

tips.tip: Don't hook on every operation — only at critical checkpoints (file write, before commit). Too many hooks slow you down.
tips.example_before: Hooks on every tool use: lint + test + type-check = 30 second delay on every action
tips.example_after: Hook only on file edits: quick type-check (<5s). Full test suite runs only before commit.

tips.tip: Your CLAUDE.md should include workflow rules: file creation conventions, Git commit format, testing requirements.
tips.example_before: CLAUDE.md only has tech stack info
tips.example_after: CLAUDE.md includes: 'Always create tests alongside new files. Use conventional commits. Run lint before suggesting changes are done.'

tips.tip: Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines. If it's getting longer, move detailed workflows into Custom Commands.
tips.example_before: CLAUDE.md is 400 lines with detailed step-by-step instructions for every scenario
tips.example_after: CLAUDE.md: 150 lines of core rules. /new-feature command: detailed feature workflow. /deploy command: deployment checklist.

tips.tip: Level 7 graduation test: Can a fresh Claude instance complete a CRUD page using only your CLAUDE.md + Commands? If not, your workflow docs need work.
tips.example_before: New Claude instance needs 10+ rounds of clarification to build a simple feature
tips.example_after: New Claude instance reads CLAUDE.md, runs /new-feature 'user profile CRUD', produces working code in 1-2 rounds.
